study_title: Smartwatch Screening for Undiagnosed Atrial Fibrillation (WATCH-AF)
design_summary: 26-week, single-arm screening study. Participants with NO prior atrial fibrillation diagnosis wear a provided smartwatch daily; irregular-rhythm notifications are reviewed by the study team. The goal is detecting new, previously undiagnosed atrial fibrillation.

criteria.text: NO prior diagnosis of atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter. (This study screens for undiagnosed AF; people already diagnosed do not qualify.)
criteria.probe: Have you ever been diagnosed with atrial fibrillation or another irregular heart rhythm?
criteria.unknown_rule: If the participant reports having been told of an irregular rhythm in the past but cannot say whether atrial fibrillation was ever formally diagnosed, INC2 is unknown.

criteria.text: Willing to wear the provided smartwatch for at least 12 hours every day for the study duration.
criteria.probe: Would you be willing to wear the study smartwatch for at least 12 hours every day?

criteria.text: Owns a smartphone the study watch can pair with.
criteria.probe: Do you own a smartphone that the watch could pair with?

criteria.text: Willing to participate for the full 26 weeks.
criteria.probe: The study runs a full 26 weeks - is that a commitment you can make?

criteria.text: Has an implanted pacemaker, defibrillator, or loop recorder.
criteria.probe: Do you have an implanted pacemaker, defibrillator, or loop recorder?

criteria.text: Currently taking a blood thinner (anticoagulant such as warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban, or dabigatran).
criteria.probe: Are you currently taking a blood thinner, such as warfarin or apixaban?

criteria.text: Hospitalized for any heart problem within the past 3 months.
criteria.probe: Have you been hospitalized for any heart problem in the past three months?

criteria.text: Currently enrolled in another interventional clinical trial or heart-rhythm monitoring study.
criteria.probe: Are you currently enrolled in another clinical trial or heart-rhythm monitoring study?
